candidates.why: Explicit top-line revenue label and matches total sales/revenue concept.
Direct sales/revenue line; same values as Total Revenue in preview, so it is a valid top-line label that may be used in some years.

variables.notes: Using a priority list of top-line revenue labels; both appear to represent the same revenue line in different possible presentations.

candidates.why: Best available direct-cost line corresponding to cost of sales / cost of goods sold; appears to capture purchased goods/services used in operations.

variables.notes: No COGS candidates were provided. Selected 'Purchase of Goods and Services' from the full preview because it is the closest direct production/service cost line, but manual review is needed to confirm it is the intended cost-of-sales measure rather than only part of operating costs.

candidates.why: Operating overhead-type expense bucket; not a total/subtotal and distinct from depreciation/amortization.
Operating expense but appears transaction-related/nonrecurring rather than core SG&A, so less preferred.
Classic SG&A/overhead component and a strong fit for operating overhead.

candidates.row_label: External Costs of Borrowing Corporate Bonds
candidates.why: Borrowing cost / finance-related item, so should be excluded from SG&A.
Headcount metric on balance sheet, not an expense row, so not SG&A.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A total row is present. Selected the best overhead component rows and excluded finance-related, balance-sheet, balancing, and clearly noncore transaction-related rows.
No clear research and development expense line appears on the provided statements.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses on liabilities to credit institutions
candidates.why: Specific interest expense line, but only one component of total interest costs.
Interest expense component, but not total interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses on other long-term liabilities
candidates.why: Interest expense component, but not total interest expense.
Finance-related but broader than interest and not preferred when clearer labels exist.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure duplicating lease interest concept, not preferred as main reported line.
Closest single reported finance-cost line and preferable to isolated interest components when later code expects one line item rather than a sum of components.

variables.notes: Candidate list only contained component interest rows plus other financial expenses, but XINT should be a single reported line item rather than multiple components. Chose 'Financial costs' from the full preview as the closest single line covering interest/finance cost; manual review recommended because it may include non-interest items. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Financial costs

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to parent companys share- holders
candidates.why: Preferred owner/parent equity line that excludes non-controlling interests, preventing double counting with MIB.
Total equity includes non-controlling interests in some years, so less preferred for BE when a parent-equity line exists.
Balancing row, not an economic equity line.
Balancing row, not an economic equity line.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing row, not equity.
Total balance sheet, not book equity.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to parent companys share- holders

variables.notes: Selected parent-company equity to exclude non-controlling interests from BE.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation item, not balance sheet minority equity.
Income statement comprehensive income allocation item, not preferred over balance sheet NCI for MIB.
Direct balance sheet non-controlling interest line; preferred for MIB.
Equivalent balance sheet minority interest label and valid fallback.
Income statement allocation item, not balance sheet minority equity.

variables.notes: Using balance sheet minority-interest labels as priority list; avoids mixing income statement allocation lines with equity balances.
